author: barnabasbusa
content: lets maybe take this into a thread

author: edgl_dev
content: i guess this is not about  lighthouse-ethrex-1?

author: 0xunclebill
content:
no, it is devnet-5 but this is just a random node i spun up 

```
participants:
  - el_type: ethrex
    el_image: ethpandaops/ethrex:glamsterdam-devnet-5
    cl_type: lighthouse
    cl_image: ethpandaops/lighthouse:glamsterdam-devnet-5
    cl_extra_params: ["--ignore-ws-check"]
    validator_count: 0

network_params:
  network: glamsterdam-devnet-5

checkpoint_sync_enabled: true
checkpoint_sync_url: https://checkpoint-sync.glamsterdam-devnet-5.ethpandaops.io

additional_services:
  - dora

global_log_level: debug

port_publisher:
  additional_services:
    enabled: true
```

author: edgl_dev
content: can you check by any chance if its --syncmode=full or its trying to snap sync? i merged snap/2 to it and maybe it causes issues

author: 0xunclebill
content:
ah this doesnt seem to be an ethrex issue. i think theres a peer(s) somewhere serving bad by range responses for some  epochs,  which causes us to ban peers. we just happened to hit that a few times running ethrex and not nethermind.

was able to sync to head with ethrex now

author: edgl_dev
content: good to know, if anything seems wrong with ethrex lmk!

author: 0xunclebill
content: might have been the fucked up prysm nodes? since the wipe i havent seen any peers serving us bad data
